rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Preserve AADL architecture by only using visible identifiers in the target scope
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>` for branching logic
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
generic_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, for example `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic in AGREE contracts
Use AGREE scalar types `int`, `real`, and `bool` for declarations
Align helper declaration types with the expression type in comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE `bool` in scope
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in contracts
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in implementation annexes without concrete bounds or visible relations
